title: Sports - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Sports Mhatre’s hamstring blow deepens Chennai’s IPL crisis By Reuters The 18-year-old has been the most prolific batter for five-time champions Chennai, who dropped to seventh place in the 10-team league after Saturday’s defeat by Sunrisers Hyderabad. Dhami, Kakshapati win at Immortal Fighting Championship in India By Nayak Paudel Two Nepali fighters, Sangita Dhami and Kunjan Kakshapati, defeated
content:
Sports Mhatre’s hamstring blow deepens Chennai’s IPL crisis By Reuters The 18-year-old has been the most prolific batter for five-time champions Chennai, who dropped to seventh place in the 10-team league after Saturday’s defeat by Sunrisers Hyderabad. Dhami, Kakshapati win at Immortal Fighting Championship in India By Nayak Paudel Two Nepali fighters, Sangita Dhami and Kunjan Kakshapati, defeated Indian fighters Aarya Choudhary and Inzamam Ul Huck, respectively, in the inaugural edition of the tournament held in Bengaluru on Saturday night. Nepal begin ICC Women’s T20I Challenge Trophy with loss By Nayak Paudel The Indu Barma-led side lost to the USA by 44 runs at Gahanga Cricket Stadium in Rwanda on Saturday. Santoshi Shrestha rebounds from defeat to break record, retain Kantipur Half-Marathon title By Binod Pandey A month after her first domestic loss in years, Shrestha overcomes doubt and fatigue with a tactical run to reclaim her crown in record time. Arjun Kulung Rai wins his fourth 100-mile title at 6th Manjushree Trail Race By Nayak Paudel Rai won the 100 miler in the second, fourth and fifth editions. He finished the 165-km-long race around the Kathmandu Valley rim in the sixth edition at 26 hours 37 minutes 49 seconds. Rokaya and Shrestha win Kantipur Half Marathon titles By Binod Pandey & Dhruva Tuladhar The 14th edition of the racing event drew a record turnout of around 4,000 participants across multiple categories. Kantipur Half Marathon kicks off with record participation By Binod Pandey Around 4,000 runners take part in 14th edition as organisers introduce timing chips and ensure equal prize money for winners. Army win Men’s PM Cup after five years By Nayak Paudel Tribhuvan Army Club defeated Sudurpashchim by five wickets in the final on Friday to win Nepal’s premier one-day cricket tournament for the third time. Associate El Clásico returns under lights at TU Stadium By Nayak Paudel A two-match T20I series between Nepal and the UAE starts on April 20, and it will mark the beginning of a month-long cricketing activity at the home of Nepali cricket. The games got more exciting with CAN revealing more diverse squads for T20Is and ODIs. How the US could clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz By US deploys drones and robotic systems to clear Hormuz mines, but operation remains slow and exposed to Iranian threats. Airee to lead Nepal T20Is, Paudel retains ODI captaincy By Binod Pandey CAN split leadership roles for UAE T20Is and League 2 tri-series against UAE and Oman. Nepal’s Dhami and Kakshapati step into international MMA spotlight By Nayak Paudel Sangita Dhami, a former South Asian Games wrestling gold medallist, and Kunjan Kakshapati aim for crucial wins in Bengaluru, India, as they build toward bigger MMA opportunities. 4,000 runners sign up for Kantipur Half Marathon By Post Report Record turnout marks growing running culture, KMG says. PM Cup 2026: Records tumble as Sudurpashchim reach historic final By Nayak Paudel From Arjun Kumal’s 500+ runs to Aasif Sheikh’s first-ever double century, the eighth Men’s PM Cup saw stars shine and delivered a breakthrough for provincial cricket ahead of the final. Nepal eye title as women’s team heads to Rwanda for T20I Challenge Trophy By Nayak Paudel The team has spent sufficient time with new head coach Harshal Pathak. With skipper Indu Barma back from injury, Nepal aim to win the title. Nepali cricketers continue to raise questions about team selection process By Nayak Paudel On Tuesday, Karnali’s star pacer Anuj Chanara quit the team citing unfair selection process. This was only the latest in a series of controversies over transparency in both domestic teams and the national setup. FIFA, AFC press NSC to lift ANFA suspension, resume election process By Himesh World football bodies warn of possible suspension if interference persists, reject call for tier-based polls. New trial over soccer legend Maradona’s death begins in Argentina By Reuters A court in ​San Isidro, near Buenos Aires, will hear testimony from just under 100 witnesses ​as it tries Maradona’s medical team over alleged negligence in the death ⁠of the 1986 World Cup champion. Aasif Sheikh’s double century steals the spotlight at Men’s PM Cup By Nayak Paudel The eighth edition of the Men’s PM Cup marked the tournament’s first-ever double century on Monday. The day also saw the most sixes in an innings and the highest totals. Aasif Sheikh becomes first player to hit double hundred in domestic cricket By Binod Pandey The opener struck 208 off 142 balls in the PM Cup, breaking Pradeep Airee’s long-standing record.

The day also saw the most sixes in an innings and the highest totals. By Reuters

The 18-year-old has been the most prolific batter for five-time champions Chennai, who dropped to seventh place in the 10-team league after Saturday’s defeat by Sunrisers Hyderabad.

By Nayak Paudel

Two Nepali fighters, Sangita Dhami and Kunjan Kakshapati, defeated Indian fighters Aarya Choudhary and Inzamam Ul Huck, respectively, in the inaugural edition of the tournament held in Bengaluru on Saturday night.

By Nayak Paudel

The Indu Barma-led side lost to the USA by 44 runs at Gahanga Cricket Stadium in Rwanda on Saturday.

By Binod Pandey

A month after her first domestic loss in years, Shrestha overcomes doubt and fatigue with a tactical run to reclaim her crown in record time.

By Nayak Paudel

Rai won the 100 miler in the second, fourth and fifth editions. He finished the 165-km-long race around the Kathmandu Valley rim in the sixth edition at 26 hours 37 minutes 49 seconds.

By Binod Pandey & Dhruva Tuladhar

The 14th edition of the racing event drew a record turnout of around 4,000 participants across multiple categories.

By Binod Pandey

Around 4,000 runners take part in 14th edition as organisers introduce timing chips and ensure equal prize money for winners.

By Nayak Paudel

Tribhuvan Army Club defeated Sudurpashchim by five wickets in the final on Friday to win Nepal’s premier one-day cricket tournament for the third time.

By Nayak Paudel

A two-match T20I series between Nepal and the UAE starts on April 20, and it will mark the beginning of a month-long cricketing activity at the home of Nepali cricket. The games got more exciting with CAN revealing more diverse squads for T20Is and ODIs.

By

CAN split leadership roles for UAE T20Is and League 2 tri-series against UAE and Oman.

By Nayak Paudel

Sangita Dhami, a former South Asian Games wrestling gold medallist, and Kunjan Kakshapati aim for crucial wins in Bengaluru, India, as they build toward bigger MMA opportunities.

By Post Report

Record turnout marks growing running culture, KMG says.

By Nayak Paudel

From Arjun Kumal’s 500+ runs to Aasif Sheikh’s first-ever double century, the eighth Men’s PM Cup saw stars shine and delivered a breakthrough for provincial cricket ahead of the final.

By Nayak Paudel

The team has spent sufficient time with new head coach Harshal Pathak. With skipper Indu Barma back from injury, Nepal aim to win the title.

By Nayak Paudel

On Tuesday, Karnali’s star pacer Anuj Chanara quit the team citing unfair selection process. This was only the latest in a series of controversies over transparency in both domestic teams and the national setup.

By Himesh

World football bodies warn of possible suspension if interference persists, reject call for tier-based polls.

By Reuters

A court in ​San Isidro, near Buenos Aires, will hear testimony from just under 100 witnesses ​as it tries Maradona’s medical team over alleged negligence in the death ⁠of the 1986 World Cup champion.

By Nayak Paudel

The eighth edition of the Men’s PM Cup marked the tournament’s first-ever double century on Monday. The day also saw the most sixes in an innings and the highest totals.

By Binod Pandey

The opener struck 208 off 142 balls in the PM Cup, breaking Pradeep Airee’s long-standing record.
